name: chimera
description: Hybrid production scraper. Cameleon pilots; chimera executes.

probe.summary: Security reconnaissance: TLS cert, headers, cookies, WAF vendors, CSP/HSTS, risk_score. Cameleon SHOULD call this before any /run-tool to choose the right tool and proxy tier.

force.description: Bypass 24h cache and force a fresh probe.

scrapy.summary: Fast HTTP scraping for APIs and static HTML. First choice when risk score is low.

spider.description: Which built-in spider to run. api_json for JSON APIs (light parsing); adaptive for HTML pages driven by CSS selectors.

selectors.description: (adaptive only) Map of field_name → CSS selector. Each yielded item carries these fields.

item_selector.description: (adaptive only, optional) CSS selector that scopes selectors to repeating elements (e.g. li.product). Omit to apply selectors to the whole document.

headers.description: HTTP headers added to every outgoing request (Cookie, User-Agent, Authorization, etc).

settings.description: Per-job overrides of Scrapy settings (DOWNLOAD_DELAY, CONCURRENT_REQUESTS, USER_AGENT, ...). Merged on top of tools/scrapy_runner/project/settings.py.

respect_robots.description: Whether to obey robots.txt. Default true; cameleon may flip to false on internal/authorized targets only.

proxy.description: Proxy URL (http://user:pass@host:port). Informational only at the bridge level — proxy plumbing lands in Session 2.

session_id.description: Session identifier: requests sharing the same session_id reuse the same fingerprint profile and proxy (sticky UA+proxy via SessionManager Redis). TTL 30 min. Functional as of Step 2.4.

firecrawl.summary: JS rendering and multi-page crawling via self-hosted Firecrawl + Playwright microservice.

crawl4ai.summary: Real Chromium/Firefox rendering with LLM-ready markdown output and JsonCssExtractionStrategy.

screenshot.summary: Ultimate fallback. Returns full-page PNG; OCR/extraction is done by cameleon, not chimera.

bypass_waf.summary: FlareSolverr wrapper for Cloudflare-class challenges. Used sparingly.

datacenter.summary: Cheap, high throughput. Default for low-risk targets.

residential.summary: Real ISP IPs. Used when risk_score >= 0.5.

mobile.summary: 4G mobile carriers. Highest trust, lowest throughput.

endpoints.POST /run-tool: Enqueue a scraping job. Returns {job_id, status}.
endpoints.GET /result/{id}: Structured JSON result for a finished job.
endpoints.GET /probe/{domain}: Synchronous reconnaissance probe — recommended before /run-tool. Lands in Step 2.1.
endpoints.GET /risk/{domain}: Aggregated risk history for a domain.
